Strip_Summary: Cop in patrol car at night, dispatch call coming in, knowing the neighborhood
POV from patrol car - MDT screen glowing blue on dash, hand near holster, already knowing
Bold white text on dark background: 'Not because you know them.'
Streetlight catching crooked-parked car hood at curb, cop scanning ahead without looking
Cop checking address again on MDT, zip code revealing everything dispatcher did not say
Bold white text on dark background: 'You don't protect people. You sort them.'
Young recruit in training room, instructor teaching five words as formula before teaching aim
Recruit learning sentence like field-stripping weapon, eyes closed, without thinking
Training room smelling of floor wax and old coffee, instructor writing on board without underlining
Thirty recruits in room, nobody asking when to use it, answer is never
First patrol day, holster pulling at hip, weight is real on the belt
Walking slower because of weapon weight, every step heavier on left side
Partner driving patrol car silently, adjusting mirror twice, saying something about weather
Not remembering what partner said, radio filling the silence between them
First and second dispatch calls producing nothing, listening too carefully, waiting for different one
Close on cop hoping never to have to use weapon - any of it
Third week routine stop, hand drifting to holster unconsciously on walk back to car
Close on hand caught near holster, pulling back, brief moment of self-awareness
Partner not noticing or pretending, hand moving for unknown reason, not asking why
First draw at domestic call - man in doorway unarmed, weapon drawn anyway
Knowing he is unarmed before pulling, hand moving anyway, man freezing in doorway
Street going quiet, dog behind chain-link stopping barking, everything around stopping
Woman in doorway covering mouth without sound, everything stopping except the cop
Close on cop waiting for fear - shaking hands, racing pulse - none coming
Something else arriving instead of fear, something steady and unnamed
Holding weapon position longer than needed, putting it away feels like giving something back
Trying to explain experience once, not to anyone specific, just internally
Explanation not coming out right, stopping trying after that, silence closing
Giving measured commands on sidewalk - show hands, get on ground, each word deliberate
Space between commands for compliance, civilian complying, nobody questioning the draw
Close on hand on holster at car - not placed consciously, reaching before thinking
Pulling hand back, body camera blinking red on chest, looking at it, still looking
Drawing on different person next week, same result, they freeze, cop does not
Three weeks later drawing faster, shouting before they stop, everything stopping except cop
Same quiet feeling repeating, same stillness in hands, identical each time
Hand on holster, reaching before thinking, leaving it there this time
Commands overlapping - don't move, get down - impossible to obey both simultaneously
Hands where I can see them, no pause, no space between commands
Knowing civilian cannot finish command before next, thinking about it one second, then not
Firing then shouting drop the weapon afterward, no weapon present
Audio without timestamps in court, review board meeting, three behind desk with folders
Board member clicking pen seven times, asking what happened, cop saying five words
Board nodding, clearing cop in forty minutes - forty minutes for a life
Cop at precinct desk writing furtive movement - two words meaning a man moved hands
Same two words written before, different person, not remembering when first time was
Partner seeing what happened versus what was written - different events
Partner looking at report, looking at ceiling, signing it without speaking
Firing again two months later, not needing to, report saying otherwise, always otherwise
Shooting survivor filing suit dismissed, cop never learning their name
Highest shooting count in precinct, sergeant calling cop proactive
Rookie watching cop draw weapon, frozen and silent, cop not looking at them
Driving through neighborhood off duty, someone crossing street, everything stopping on arrival
Five words coming out before question finishes, automatic, no thought required
Words coming out involuntary like a cough, body producing without mind
Close on body camera clicking off, cop not looking at it anymore
See, reach, fire - no command, no pause, no thought, sequence compressed to reflex
Tonight different, wet sidewalk from sprinkler running too long, tension changed
Close on tight jaw, not sure when the tension started, body on its own
Ordering show your ID, civilian reaching slowly, mouth opening Hands- then firing
Close on ground - ID card open, photo facing up, fallen from hand
Bold text on dark background: 'It was already in their hand.'
Close on cop standing over fallen civilian - told them to reach, told them
Something skipping in chest, not pain, just a skip, then gone
Rookie frozen behind, cop fired first, rookie fired after, contagion sequence
Rookie did not decide - cop's decision became theirs, contagious shooting
Rookie staring with wide eyes and hands still up, raw recognition
Same look cop had once in training room before learning five words
Before the five words, not remembering when that look was lost
Precinct office, fluorescent light, and then it is over, just like that
Teaching rookie the five words, sitting across desk in precinct office
Fluorescent light humming above, not knowing how long it has been doing that
Rookie saying five words back, same words, sounding hollow in new voice
Wrong in their voice - too new, too clean, like hearing own recorded voice
Close on cop trying to remember first time meaning the words, searching for feeling
Not the words but the feeling - cannot remember, emptiness where memory should be
Rookie using five words next month, same phrase same weight different person
Hearing about it on radio, not feeling anything, system replicating
Looking at report on desk, recognizing own handwriting, recognizing the words
Bold text: 'You don't recognize the person they describe.'
Stack of files, words blurring together, same sentences same structure different names
Blinking, words coming back into focus, continuing to read mechanically
Close on file corner - ID photo stapled, staring at face, not remembering it
Maybe remembering, not sure anymore, recognition dissolving
Close on report - one wrong detail visible, pen untouched, choosing not to fix
Writing five words at bottom of page, same pen same ink, mechanical formula
Bold text on dark background: 'You don't remember if you did.'
Same patrol car as opening, same MDT blue glow, same format, different person
Close on hand on holster, reaching before thinking, not noticing anymore
Not yet left car, already knowing, same as beginning, cycle sealed
Bold text on dark background: 'You didn't place it.'
